mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0 percent, higher borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and trigger equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered months despite the rate threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and consumer price index signal stagflationary pressure that erodes real wages and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in out-of-sample testing over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating real personal consumption expenditures outpace supply capacity, prompting equity markets to price in imminent monetary tightening and generate negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of late-cycle observations when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding corporate output and revenue growth, which mechanically supports higher S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains negative or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent indicates a sudden supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than half of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals a sectoral rotation away from consumer staples toward cyclical construction, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05 in a multivariate regression predicting 5-day returns over any 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, rapid growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic stabilization and boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of recovery-phase months when payroll growth exceeds 150000.
